top_stories.title: Microsoft 365 users targeted by new phishing threat that bypasses MFA
top_stories.summary: The FBI has issued a warning about Kali365, a Phishing-as-a-Service platform first observed in April 2026 that captures Microsoft 365 OAuth access tokens to bypass MFA without stealing credentials. The platform is distributed via Telegram and provides attackers with AI-generated phishing lures, automated campaign templates, real-time tracking dashboards, and OAuth token capture capabilities.

top_stories.title: Announcing The Forrester Wave™: Workforce Identity Platforms, Q2 2026
top_stories.summary: Forrester has released its Q2 2026 Wave evaluation of Workforce Identity Security Platforms, assessing and ranking major providers in the workforce identity security market. The report reflects the growing strategic importance of workforce identity security in modern cybersecurity environments.

top_stories.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
top_stories.summary: New Omdia research examines how AI agent identity management is creating distinct budget dynamics compared to traditional IAM projects. The proliferation of AI agent identities in enterprises is driving new requirements for identity management, security, and governance.

top_stories.title: Deleted Google API keys keep working for up to 23 minutes, researchers warn
top_stories.summary: Aikido Security researchers found that deleted Google API keys remain valid and continue authenticating for up to 23 minutes after deletion. This affects credential revocation practices, as attackers who obtain a leaked key can still use it to make API calls, access Gemini AI files, and view cached conversations during the post-deletion window.

top_stories.title: Versa extends zero trust principles to AI agents and MCP workflows
top_stories.summary: Versa has introduced a patent-pending zero trust architecture for the Model Context Protocol (MCP), applying identity-based validation to AI agent actions before execution. The system validates every AI-generated action against user identity, role-based access controls, and system policies, with optional human approval gates configured by administrators.

news.title: npm Adds 2FA-Gated Publishing and Package Install Controls Against Supply Chain Attacks
news.summary: GitHub has introduced staged publishing to npm, requiring maintainers to complete a 2FA challenge before packages become publicly available for installation. The feature is now generally available and adds human-gated approval controls to the npm publishing workflow as a supply chain security measure.

news.title: Proton Pass adds monitored credential sharing for AI agents
news.summary: Proton Pass has added AI access tokens that enable monitored credential sharing with AI agents, requiring agents to provide a reason for access requests. The feature is available across paid Proton Pass tiers and allows users to grant AI agents access to selected password vault items while monitoring their activity.

news.title: Security hardening to the autoupgrade process for Microsoft Entra Connect and Microsoft Entra Connect Health - Microsoft Entra ID
news.summary: Microsoft is implementing security hardening changes to the autoupgrade process for Microsoft Entra Connect and Microsoft Entra Connect Health. These updates affect the automated update mechanisms for the hybrid identity synchronization tools that connect on-premises Active Directory environments to Microsoft Entra ID.

news.title: Microsoft recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Workforce Identity Security Platforms
news.summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Workforce Identity Security Platforms, Q2 2026. The company received the highest scores in both the current offering and strategy categories in the analyst evaluation.

news.title: Identity security for AI agents: The proliferation challenge
news.summary: The article addresses the challenge of securing AI agent identities, distinguishing them from human identities and traditional non-human identities (NHIs). It focuses on how identity teams must adapt security foundations to manage the nondeterministic behavior of AI agents as part of broader AI adoption.

news.title: Most data breaches start with a stolen password. Here’s how to fix that
news.summary: The article addresses the widespread problem of poor password hygiene in organizations, including credential reuse, sharing, and insecure storage. It focuses on authentication vulnerabilities and how to remediate them, which is directly relevant to IAM professionals managing identity and access controls.

news.title: GCC firms rethink cyber defences as AI phishing surges
news.summary: Security leaders across Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) firms are accelerating investments in identity security and rethinking trust models in response to AI-powered phishing campaigns expanding beyond email into collaboration tools like Teams, calendars, and AI platforms. The shift reflects growing adoption of zero trust frameworks as attack surfaces broaden across enterprise identity and communication channels.

news.title: OktaがClaude Compliance APIと統合、Claude環境内のアイデンティティ保護を強化
news.summary: Okta has announced integration with Claude's Compliance API to strengthen identity protection within Claude environments. The integration involves Okta's Identity Security Posture Management (ISPM) product working within Anthropic's Claude platform.

news.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
news.summary: Microsoft has disclosed two actively exploited vulnerabilities in Microsoft Defender, including a privilege escalation flaw (CVE-2026-41091, CVSS 7.8) that can grant attackers SYSTEM privileges. The flaw involves improper link resolution before file access, alongside a separate denial-of-service vulnerability.

news.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
news.summary: An analysis examines how a single cached AWS access key on a Windows machine can expose up to 98% of a company's cloud entities, representing a critical identity-based attack path. The scenario illustrates how standard credential caching behavior—not misconfiguration—creates lateral movement opportunities that attackers can exploit to traverse cloud environments.

news.title: Why Okta (OKTA) Sees AI Agents as an Identity-Security Opportunity
news.summary: Okta is positioning AI agents as an opportunity within its identity security business. The article frames this in the context of the stock's recent underperformance and potential turnaround investment thesis.

news.title: WSO2 Accelerates Agentic Enterprise Adoption with New Agent Identity, Forward Deployed Engineers, and Expanded Delivery Partner Ecosystem
news.summary: WSO2 has expanded its Agent Fabric platform with new agent identity capabilities designed for AI-native enterprise environments. The update includes AI agent identity management features alongside forward deployed engineering services and an expanded delivery partner ecosystem.

news.title: Semperis Takes Center Stage at Infosec Europe 2026
news.summary: Semperis, an identity-driven cyber resilience vendor, will take center stage at Infosec Europe 2026. The event will feature a panel with Simon Hodgkinson, Grace Cassy, Sarah Armstrong-Smith, and Jesse McGraw discussing "Midnight in the War Room."

news.title: Protecting collaboration with behavior-based access controls
news.summary: The article discusses behavior-based access controls as a security approach for protecting modern collaboration sessions. It positions this method as a potential improvement over traditional security tools for securing collaborative environments.

news.title: Implement SAML SSO Authentication in Laravel Filament with Socialite
news.summary: This article provides a technical tutorial on implementing SAML SSO authentication in Laravel Filament using the Socialite library and SAML2 provider. The guide covers authentication flow components including metadata endpoints, redirects, and callbacks for enterprise applications.

news.title: Agent AI is Coming. Are You Ready?
news.summary: Orchid Security released its "Identity Gap: Snapshot 2026" report on May 19, 2026, examining unmanaged identity elements in enterprise environments. The report found that "identity dark matter" — unseen and unmanaged identity components — now accounts for 57% of enterprise identity compared to 43% for visible, managed identity, coinciding with increased enterprise adoption of Agent AI.

news.title: Security Hub Extended expands to 21 curated partner solutions across 9 categories
news.summary: AWS expanded its Security Hub Extended plan to include 21 curated partner solutions across 9 security categories. CyberArk is listed as one of the included partners in the identity category alongside other vendors covering endpoint, email, data security, and other domains.

news.title: Provision IAM Announces Strategic Investments from Georgia's Own and InTouch Credit Unions
news.summary: Provision IAM announced strategic investments from Georgia's Own Credit Union and InTouch Credit Union, expanding its Customer Advisory Board. The investments represent credit union partnerships aimed at aligning the IAM vendor's product direction with financial sector needs.

news.title: Okta (OKTA) Could Raise Its Fiscal 2027 Guidance Amid Improved Demand And Execution, Says Barclays
news.summary: Barclays analyst commentary suggests Okta could raise its fiscal 2027 guidance based on improved demand and execution. The note highlights Okta's position among recommended cloud stocks with a cited upside potential of 21.50% and a short float of 5.03%.

news.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
news.summary: CISA accidentally exposed secrets and credentials in a GitHub repository named "Private-CISA" that was publicly accessible since November 2025. The leaked repository contained sensitive credentials, raising concerns about credential management practices at a federal cybersecurity agency.

news.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
news.summary: A set of vulnerabilities dubbed "Claw Chain" were discovered and patched in the OpenClaw AI agent framework, enabling attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence. The flaws affect deployments of the rapidly growing AI agent framework, with the specific attack vectors involving credential theft and privilege escalation.
